articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: 'Chucky' Lozano goal leads Mexico to win over Ghana
article.description: Goals from PSV Eindhoven's Hirving "Chucky" Lozano and Cruz Azul's Uriel Antuna clinched a 2-0 friendly victory for Mexico against Ghana on Saturday.
article.linkText: 'Chucky' Lozano leads Mexico to win over Ghana

images.name: Mexico celeb vs. Ghana_20231014 [1296x729]
images.caption: Hirving "Chucky" Lozano's goal led Mexico to a friendly win over Ghana in Charlotte, North Carolina.
images.alt: Hirving Lozano #22 of Mexico celebrates after scoring his team's first goal during the friendly match against Ghana at Bank of America Stadium on October 14, 2023 in Charlotte, North Carolina.

peers.caption: Hirving "Chucky" Lozano's goal led Mexico to a friendly win over Ghana in Charlotte, North Carolina.
peers.alt: Hirving Lozano #22 of Mexico celebrates after scoring his team's first goal during the friendly match against Ghana at Bank of America Stadium on October 14, 2023 in Charlotte, North Carolina.

article.story:
Goals from PSV Eindhoven's Hirving "Chucky" Lozano and Cruz Azul's Uriel Antuna clinched a 2-0 friendly victory for Mexico against Ghana on Saturday.

Held in front of a packed house at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte, North Carolina, the international exhibition had plenty of intensity in the first half.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

With 18 fouls in total during the opening 45 minutes, there were no shortage of stop-and-start moments between the two nations. During one of those dead-ball situations, Ghana were forced to make an early substitution in the 28th minute after an injury to Celta Vigo defender Joseph Aidoo led to Clermont's Alidu Seidu coming on in his place.

Following a scoreless first half, Mexico appeared more galvanized coming out after the midway break. El Tri pushed forward, eventually earning a free-kick opportunity that set the team up for their opening goal.

After Luis Chávez quickly sent the ball to Lozano, the winger pounced on the opportunity by cutting into the 18-yard box and sending a low shot into Ghana's net in the 57th minute.

Given additional boosts from second-half substitutions, Mexico soon doubled their lead. Substitutes Cesar Huerta and Antuna linked up during a dangerous surge forward, eventually culminating in a through-ball from Huerta that provided Antuna with an opportunity to score El Tri's second goal of the night in the 72nd minute.

Mexico had little trouble defending Ghana's attempts at a comeback to maintain the 2-0 scoreline by the final whistle.

Mexico will close out the international break with a friendly against Germany on Tuesday, while Ghana will face the United States on the same day.

keyEvents.text: Thomas Partey (Ghana) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Joseph Aidoo (Ghana) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Ghana. Alidu Seidu replaces Joseph Aidoo because of an injury.
Substitution, Ghana. Salis Abdul Samed replaces Thomas Partey.
Jorge Sánchez (Mexico) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Mexico 1, Ghana 0. Hirving Lozano (Mexico) right footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Luis Chávez   following a set piece situation.
Substitution, Mexico. César Huerta replaces Hirving Lozano.
Substitution, Mexico. Uriel Antuna replaces Orbelín Pineda.
Substitution, Ghana. Iñaki Williams replaces Antoine Semenyo.
Substitution, Ghana. Ernest Nuamah replaces Joseph Paintsil.
Substitution, Ghana. Jordan Ayew replaces Kingsley Schindler.
Goal!  Mexico 2, Ghana 0. Uriel Antuna (Mexico) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by César Huerta with a through ball.
Substitution, Mexico. Luis Romo replaces Edson Álvarez.
Substitution, Mexico. Santiago Giménez replaces Raúl Jiménez.
Substitution, Mexico. Sebastián Córdova replaces Érick Sánchez.
